summary: Phase 2 Value Discovery testing. Security tab removal, value question filtering, and ValueDiscoveryDrawer all verified. Found 1 integration bug: 'Spreadsheets for IPAM' chip not appearing in Optimize tab due to format mismatch (answers stored as CSV strings but tryIncludes() expects JSON arrays).

ui_bugs.issue: Context chips for ipam-0, idns-0, dhcp-0 (Optimize tab) and ipam-9, ipam-11, ipam-13 (Accelerate tab) never trigger. GridMultiSelect stores answers as CSV strings ('Spreadsheets') but tryIncludes() uses JSON.parse() which throws on CSV format → returns false always. Same issue for hasValues() on cloud/integrations/automation triggers.
ui_bugs.rca: tryIncludes(a['ipam-0'], 'Spreadsheets') calls JSON.parse('Spreadsheets') → SyntaxError → returns false. Fix: change tryIncludes to also support CSV format: try JSON.parse first, if fail check if string includes the value directly or split by comma.

action_items: Fix tryIncludes() in ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx: handle both JSON array format AND CSV string format. GridMultiSelect stores values as 'Spreadsheets' or 'Spreadsheets, Microsoft' (CSV), but the function tries JSON.parse() only. Suggested fix: try JSON.parse first, on exception fall back to val.includes(str) or val.split(',').some(v => v.trim().includes(str))
Fix hasValues() in ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx similarly for ipam-9, ipam-11, ipam-13 which are also stored as CSV from GridMultiSelect
Note: Protect badge '1' is showing because ni-3=25000 (iot trigger), not because sec-1 was answered (sec-1 is null). The badge visual requirement passes but through a different mechanism than stated.
critical_code_review_comments: ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx tryIncludes(): expects JSON array format but answers from GridMultiSelect are stored as comma-separated strings. The catch-all 'return false' silently swallows the format mismatch.
ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx hasValues(): same issue with ipam-9 (Cloud Platform), ipam-11 (3rd Party Integrations), ipam-13 (Orchestration Tools) - all stored as CSV, not JSON arrays.

Test note: sec-1 answer is null in the Customer test record, so the 'sec-rpz' trigger for Protect is NOT firing. The Protect badge '1' fires due to ni-3=25000 (iot trigger). This could be confusing if sec-1 was supposed to pre-populate it.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (12/13 tests pass - context chips broken due to format mismatch)

test_results.drawer_3_tabs: PASS - Optimize, Accelerate, Protect tabs all present

test_results.optimize_spreadsheets_chip: FAIL - 'Spreadsheets for IPAM' chip NOT shown in Optimize tab (format mismatch bug)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data. ni-3 remains at 25000 from previous testing sessions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Customer 'Customer' (ID: ebd25fb6-bba1-48f0-819f-2da580ee46e0) used for testing. ipam-0='Spreadsheets' (CSV format), ni-3='25000', sec-1=null. Protect badge '1' fires due to iot trigger (ni-3>0), not sec-rpz. The context chips for Optimize (ipam-0 Spreadsheets, idns-0 Microsoft/BIND, dhcp-0 Microsoft) and Accelerate (ipam-9 cloud, ipam-11 integrations, ipam-13 automation) all silently fail because tryIncludes/hasValues expect JSON.parse() but values are stored as CSV. The fix is in ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx lines 59-67.

rca_of_bug.bug: Spreadsheets for IPAM chip not appearing in Value Discovery Drawer Optimize tab
rca_of_bug.reproduction: 1. Open Customer with Spreadsheets selected as Current DNS Platform (ipam-0='Spreadsheets'). 2. Open Value Discovery Drawer. 3. Select Optimize tab. Expected: 'Spreadsheets for IPAM' chip visible. Actual: No chip.
rca_of_bug.root_cause: ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx tryIncludes() does JSON.parse(val) to detect values. GridMultiSelect component stores answers as comma-separated strings (e.g., 'Spreadsheets', 'Microsoft, BIND'). JSON.parse('Spreadsheets') throws SyntaxError, caught silently, returns false. All chip triggers relying on tryIncludes() and hasValues() will fail.
rca_of_bug.mitigation: In ValueDiscoveryDrawer.jsx, update tryIncludes to: function tryIncludes(val, str) { if (!val) return false; try { const arr = JSON.parse(val); return Array.isArray(arr) && arr.some(v => v.includes(str)); } catch { return val.split(',').some(v => v.trim().includes(str)); } } Similarly update hasValues() to: function hasValues(val) { if (!val) return false; try { const arr = JSON.parse(val); return Array.isArray(arr) && arr.length > 0; } catch { return val.trim().length > 0; } }
